stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a major LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: The White House has accelerated the timeline for transitioning to quantum-resistant cryptography.
The urgency is driven by significant national security risks posed by current cryptographic standards against future quantum computers.
This mandates a rapid industry-wide adoption of post-quantum cryptography (PQC).

stories.title: Usbliter8: an A12/A13 SecureROM Exploit
stories.author: givinguflac
bullet_points: The news concerns a security vulnerability ('Usbliter8') affecting Apple's A12 and A13 SecureROM chips.
Exploits targeting SecureROM are highly significant as they represent deep-level hardware vulnerabilities.
This topic is of major relevance to cybersecurity, hardware security, and Apple ecosystem integrity.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, suggesting a modern architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and lower memory usage for models.
The updated engine also enables models to generate higher quality outputs.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an ongoing development and performance enhancement.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Oracle is undertaking massive, debt-fueled investments in data center infrastructure to support its AI initiatives.
The company is simultaneously implementing significant workforce reductions, with 21,000 layoffs reported.
This signals a strategic pivot where massive capital expenditure on AI hardware is being financed alongside cost-cutting measures.

stories.title: SAFARI: Scaling Long Horizon Agentic Fault Attribution via Active Investigation
stories.author: Erin Babinsky
bullet_points: Addresses the critical problem of diagnosing failures in complex, long-horizon autonomous agents.
Current methods fail because agent execution traces exceed the context window limits of LLMs.
Introduces SAFARI, a new framework utilizing active investigation to attribute faults without loading full trajectories into context.

stories.title: Privacy-Preserving RAG via Multi-Agent Semantic Rewriting: Achieving Confidentiality Without Compromising Contextual Fidelity
stories.author: Tao Fang
bullet_points: Addresses privacy risks in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems when handling sensitive data.
Proposes a multi-agent framework to sanitize retrieved content via semantic rewriting.
Uses specialized agents (privacy extraction, semantic analysis, reconstruction) to remove identifiers while maintaining context.

stories.title: CoorDex: Coordinating Body and Hand Priors for Continuous Dexterous Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: Introduces CoorDex, a new learning pipeline for continuous, dexterous humanoid loco-manipulation.
Addresses the limitation of traditional stop-and-go manipulation by coordinating high-dimensional body and hand control.
Enables high-DoF dexterous manipulation while the humanoid is actively moving (on the move).

stories.title: Privacy-Preserving RAG via Multi-Agent Semantic Rewriting: Achieving Confidentiality Without Compromising Contextual Fidelity
stories.author: Tao Fang
bullet_points: Proposes a multi-agent framework to enhance Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) privacy.
The system sanitizes retrieved content by using specialized agents for privacy extraction and semantic rewriting.
Aims to achieve confidentiality in sensitive LLM deployments without losing contextual fidelity.

stories.title: Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents
stories.author: Ning Ding
bullet_points: Introduces Qwen-AgentWorld, a new class of language world models designed for general agents.
The models aim to enhance agent capabilities by predicting environment dynamics based on observations and actions.
The research presents large-scale foundation models (e.g., Qwen-AgentWorld-35B-A3B) capable of simulating agentic environments.

stories.title: AdversaBench: Automated LLM Red-Teaming with Multi-Judge Confirmation and Cross-Model Transferability
stories.author: Khanak Khandelwal
bullet_points: Introduces AdversaBench, an end-to-end pipeline for automated red-teaming of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The system uses structured prompt mutation operators and a three-judge panel for reliable failure confirmation.
Evaluations cover critical LLM capabilities, including reasoning, instruction-following, and tool use.

stories.title: NatureBench: Can Coding Agents Match the Published SOTA of Nature-Family Papers?
stories.author: Kaiyan Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces NatureBench, a new cross-discipline benchmark derived from Nature-family publications.
Aims to test if AI coding agents can move from merely reproducing results to making genuine scientific discoveries.
Utilizes NatureGym, an automated pipeline to create standardized, containerized environments from source research papers.

stories.title: AGORA: An Archive-Grounded Benchmark for Agentic Workplace Document Reasoning
stories.author: Qi Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces AGORA, a new benchmark specifically designed for 'archive-grounded reasoning' in LLM agents.
Addresses the complex real-world challenge of LLMs reasoning over large, messy workplace documents, requiring evidence location and inconsistency reconciliation.
Highlights a gap in existing benchmarks by jointly testing archive-groundedness, agentic exploration, and cross-domain coverage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon devices.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google Home is enhancing its facial recognition capabilities.
The update aims to improve identification accuracy, even when users are not fully visible to the camera.
The feature expansion, starting June 23rd, applies to users tagged in the Familiar Faces library.

stories.title: CineCap: Structured Reasoning with Spatio-Temporal Anchors for Cinematographic Video Captioning
stories.author: Max Meng
bullet_points: Introduces CineCap, a new method for cinematographic video captioning.
Focuses on describing video using professional film language (e.g., camera movement, shot size).
Addresses a gap in current multimodal LLMs regarding fine-grained, film-language video understanding.

stories.title: Themis: An explainable AI-enabled framework for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback
stories.author: Dimitris Chatzpoulos
bullet_points: Introduces 'Themis,' a novel framework designed to improve Reinforcement Learning (RL) safety.
Themis uniquely combines Explainable AI (XAI) with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF).
Addresses the critical industry challenge of ensuring RL systems avoid unwanted or unsafe behaviors.

stories.title: ScaleToT: Generalizing Structured LLM Reasoning for Billion-Scale Low-Activity User Modeling
stories.author: Kun Gai
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of user modeling for billions of low-activity users lacking rich interaction histories.
Proposes ScaleToT, a method to generalize structured reasoning from a small LLM-processed subset to the entire low-activity population.
Offers a scalable solution to the high computational cost of applying LLMs to massive user bases for inference.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Longitudinal Forecasting of Alzheimer's Disease Progression Using Deep Learning
stories.author: Anala M R
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current deep learning models for Alzheimer's progression by moving beyond single-step classification.
The proposed method incorporates uncertainty awareness to model how patient evolution and forecast reliability change over time.
This probabilistic approach offers more clinically useful insights than traditional methods that treat disease stages as flat categories.

stories.title: AutoDex: An Automated Real-World System for Dexterous Grasping Data Collection
stories.author: Hanbyul Joo
bullet_points: AutoDex proposes an automated system to address the challenge of collecting large-scale, real-world data for dexterous grasping.
The system bridges the gap between slow, operator-biased teleoperation and unverified simulation data.
It automates the loop of generating candidate grasps and verifying their physical outcomes on real hardware.

stories.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
stories.author: Daniel Cohen-Or
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of mode collapse in text-to-image models, where outputs lack diversity despite high visual fidelity.
Proposes a new method for 'controlled diversity' by enforcing structure on generated samples.
Moves beyond incidental variations to allow for meaningful, design-driven control over image generation outputs.

stories.title: Open Problem: Is AdamW Effective Under Heavy-Tailed Noise?
stories.author: Lijun Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a theoretical gap in AdamW, the standard optimizer for LLMs, concerning its performance under heavy-tailed noise.
Empirical evidence suggests LLM pretraining involves heavy-tailed stochastic gradient noise, challenging current theoretical assumptions.
The work highlights that sign-based optimizers (Lion, Muon) and AdaGrad have shown promise in achieving convergence under these heavy-tailed conditions.

stories.title: Tapered Language Models
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: Challenges the standard architecture of modern language models (e.g., Transformers) which uniformly allocate parameters across all layers.
Proposes that the contribution of layers is non-uniform, suggesting later layers primarily refine the residual stream rather than performing major transformations.
Investigates whether parameter capacity allocation within deep learning models should be tapered or non-uniform.

stories.title: On the Limits of Prompt-Conditioned Language Models as General-Purpose Learners
stories.author: Jun Wang
bullet_points: Challenges the prevailing view of LLMs as truly general-purpose learners.
Proposes that language acts as a capacity-limited interface for task information.
Analyzes prompt encoding and reinterpretation using a bilevel 'cheap-talk' game framework.

stories.title: MAS-PromptBench: When Does Prompt Optimization Improve Multi-Agent LLM Systems?
stories.author: Laixi Shi
bullet_points: The research focuses on Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) using LLMs, a key area for scalable agentic AI.
System prompts are identified as a critical and accessible optimization surface for improving MAS behavior without needing model finetuning.
The paper investigates the specific conditions under which prompt optimization yields improvements in complex multi-agent setups.

stories.title: Action-BED: Task-Driven Bayesian Experimental Design with Singly Intractable Objectives
stories.author: Tom Rainforth
bullet_points: Introduces Action-BED, a new framework for Bayesian Experimental Design (BED).
Addresses the major limitation of traditional BED, which involves doubly intractable objectives.
Reformulates BED using Expected Future Loss (EFL) based on first principles decision theory for better task customization.

stories.title: The Warrant Gap: Claim-Conditioned Re-scoring for Fact-Checking
stories.author: John Collomosse
bullet_points: Addresses a critical flaw in LLM-based fact-checking: outputting 'Supports' labels when evidence doesn't actually support the claim.
Introduces SIFT, a novel method for claim-conditioned re-scoring of extracted evidence against the full claim context.
Pairs SIFT with WSP (Warranted Supports Proportion), an automated Natural Language Inference (NLI) check to improve accuracy.

stories.title: Same Lesson, Different Story: Cross-Lingual Reconstruction of Cultural Narratives in Large Language Models
stories.author: Rehab Alahmadi
bullet_points: Addresses the complexity of evaluating cultural grounding in LLMs when the same moral lesson is expressed across different cultures.
Highlights the uncertainty regarding whether LLMs preserve culturally grounded meaning across diverse narratives.
Proposes a new multilingual evaluation narrative framework to tackle this cross-cultural challenge.

stories.title: To Compare, or Not to Compare: On Methodological Practices in Evaluating Social Bias
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need to evaluate social biases in Large Language Models (LLMs) deployed in sensitive applications.
Identifies methodological fragmentation in current bias evaluation literature as a major problem leading to contradictory results.
Proposes a unified and controllable framework to standardize heterogeneous benchmarks for systematic bias contrast.

stories.title: MEMPROBE: Probing Long-Term Agent Memory via Hidden User-State Recovery
stories.author: Zhen Wang
bullet_points: The paper introduces MEMPROBE, a method to directly probe the hidden user-state within LLM agents' long-term memory.
Current evaluation of LLM memory relies heavily on indirect downstream behaviors (e.g., task success), which is deemed insufficient.
The authors advocate for evaluating long-term memory as an auditable artifact rather than solely through behavioral outcomes.

stories.title: Cross-Lingual Exploration for Parametric Knowledge
stories.author: Omri Abend
bullet_points: The research addresses the uneven accessibility of parametric knowledge within Large Language Models across different languages.
It investigates cross-lingual prompting strategies to uncover hidden factual knowledge that standard inference techniques miss.
The study identifies four key dimensions that govern how parametric knowledge is retrieved during cross-lingual exploration.

stories.title: Cloudflare Collaborates With Leading Browsers to Develop a Privacy-First Protocol For the Global Internet
stories.author: cloudflare.net via freddyb
bullet_points: Cloudflare is partnering with major browser developers.
The collaboration aims to create a new privacy-first protocol for the internet.
This initiative addresses growing concerns regarding online privacy and data handling.

stories.title: AI found 300 WordPress plugin zero-days in 72 hours. I build plugins. Here's what changed for me.
stories.author: rapls
bullet_points: AI tools were used to discover a large number (300) of zero-day vulnerabilities in WordPress plugins.
The author shares a personal reflection on how this discovery influenced their development practices.
The story highlights the increasing role of AI in security auditing within the WordPress ecosystem.

stories.title: Agents write code, but they don't remember
stories.author: lizziepika
bullet_points: Code generation capabilities are advanced, but the persistent memory of AI agents remains a significant unsolved problem.
The Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) is reportedly shifting, prioritizing 'intent' over direct code implementation.
The article discusses the risks and losses teams incur when an AI agent's reasoning process is not retained or traceable.

stories.title: Hetzner Doubled Its Prices Again. The AI Memory Crunch Is Why
stories.author: devopsdaily
bullet_points: Hetzner, a hosting provider, has increased its service prices again.
The price hike is attributed to the growing demand and resource strain caused by AI workloads.
The article suggests an 'AI Memory Crunch' is driving up infrastructure costs.
